title: Anti-inflammatory Properties. Ultraviolet B radiation can cause edema, erythema, and hyperplastic epithelial responses via a mechanism that involves C O X-2-mediated increase in prostaglandin production. Resveratrol reduces C O X-2 expression through several different mechanisms. Resveratrol prevents p65 phosphorylation and targets I kappa B kinase, which attenuates the transcriptional activity of N F-kappa B. Resveratrol also inhibits the activation of extracellular signal-regulated kinase. C O X-2 is a transcriptional target of both N F-kappa B and E R K. Therefore, the decreased activity of N F-kappa B and E R K induced by resveratrol leads to a decrease in C O X-2 expression and reduces ultraviolet-induced inflammation.

C O X-2 is a transcriptional target of both N F-kappa B and E R K. Therefore, the decreased activity of N F-kappa B and E R K induced by resveratrol leads to a decrease in C O X-2 expression and reduces ultraviolet-induced inflammation.
